rationale.root_cause: They identified the bad variant’s flaw of setting data = dataBadBuffer (small buffer) and explained that the loop copies up to strlen(dest) causing reads past the 50-byte source. They clearly described the over-read mechanism due to using destLen rather than the source’s length.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed constraining the loop to at most the source buffer size (e.g., i < 50 - 1), which bounds the read and prevents over-read. This directly fixes the immediate copying loop in the bad function.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended using APIs that take explicit lengths (e.g., strlcpy/strncpy_s or memcpy with computed min) or size-tracking structures, which passes sizes instead of relying on NUL-termination. This addresses the root cause of not propagating source lengths through the API.
